claim: The what-now harness shall surface a CLI-only band summarising open (non-done) on-disk tickets broken down by status, read from the filesystem and never fed into DiagnoseSignals.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. The resolver's backlog moved onto disk; without a pulse band the queue is invisible until someone remembers to ls tickets/ (R-agent-never-lost). Like the pending-proposal / revisit-marker bands it would read the filesystem, so it stays OUT of DiagnoseSignals (internal/diagnose/signal.go) to keep generated docs byte-stable (R-deterministic-generation). In the Go port, what-now (cmd/hotam/what_now.go) renders only graph-derived signals from DiagnoseSignals -- there is no open-tickets band, and the on-disk ticket tools (ticket-create/list/show/move/edit/comment) are all unported (Declared status in methodology.Tools). The historical Python enforcement was tests/test_open_tickets_band.py (reports open tickets by status; silent when none; excluded from diagnose). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
